hook.text: Your employer takes tax out of every paycheck. That's withholding—paying as you earn.

buildup.text: The government doesn't want to wait until April. Withholding ensures steady revenue all year.

discovery.text: When you file a tax return, you calculate the real amount owed and compare it to what was withheld.

twist.text: A big refund doesn't mean you won. It means you gave the government an interest-free loan.

climax.text: Adjusting your withholding correctly means no surprise bill—and no oversized refund.

punchline.text: A big refund means you overpaid all year.

quiz.question: What does tax withholding do?
options: Takes tax from each paycheck throughout the year
Delays all tax payment until April
Eliminates the need to file a return
